rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Manually verify the spreadsheet preserves cell formulas (facility_location_1.xlsx) since the environment could not parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable). In the memo/spreadsheet, explicitly state whether remediation purchase costs are global one-time costs or per-record (the memo's treatment is consistent with a global-bundle framing but making the assumption explicit improves reproducibility). Also include a short, explicit numeric summary table in the spreadsheet showing base-case and each feasible bundle's computed totals so reviewers can quickly cross-check.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts are present. The memo contains explicit row-level citations to R1-R3 and O1-O3 and max_budget, enumerates all remediation bundles and correctly enforces the 114000 budget, flags the unmet-demand penalty as missing and treats it as a symbolic variable p, and provides consistent sensitivity / break-even calculations (verified algebraically against the cited packet values). The memo's numerical thresholds and dominance conclusions are internally consistent with the embedded-packet values. The spreadsheet artifact exists (deterministic precheck passed) but could not be parsed programmatically in this environment; presence is established by the artifact paths and deterministic precheck.

attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Clarify in the memo and spreadsheet whether remediation options are global or per-record (the memo treats O1/O2/O3 as global changes, but an explicit statement and sheet reference would remove ambiguity). Also, include explicit sheet and cell references in the memo (e.g., 'Combinations sheet, cell B10') and ensure the spreadsheet file remains programmatically inspectable (openpyxl preview failed here).
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts are present. The memo explicitly cites all embedded-packet values (R1,R2,R3; O1,O2,O3; max_budget) and flags the missing unmet-demand penalty as needs_review. Derived metrics in the memo are arithmetically consistent with the packet (total capacity 79, total demand 139, baseline shortfall 60; fixed_cost_k sum = 20 -> $20,000). The remediation feasibility enumeration enforces budget=114,000 (only none, O1, O2, O3 singletons are budget-feasible; all pairs exceed budget). Sensitivity analysis is provided via break-even penalty thresholds computed as remediation cost divided by shortfall reduction (numbers shown in memo match those calculations). The memo states the formal optimum is 'needs_review' pending the missing penalty and gives a provisional recommendation with rationale. The trace indicates the workbook was generated with preserved formulas and scenario sheets (Analysis, Combinations, Recommendation), though an external preview of XLSX formulas was not parseable in this environment. Overall, observable evidence satisfies the rubric success criteria: artifacts present, formulas preserved (per trace), embedded-packet values used only, budget enforced, missing parameter flagged, and sensitivity analysis present.
